content.seo_title: For Founders: User Onboarding to Solve 'Can't Finish Projects'
content.seo_description: Learn how to implement user onboarding to overcome project stagnation. A beginner-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code for faster SaaS delivery.
content.answer_block: If you struggle to finish projects, it is likely due to a lack of user validation. Implement a high-friction onboarding flow to qualify users early. By forcing users to interact with your core value proposition immediately, you gain the data needed to ship, iterate, and monetize faster.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS is littered with 'almost-finished' projects that never saw a single paying user. You wake up, open your IDE, and stare at a half-baked dashboard, paralyzed by feature creep and the crushing weight of missing a clear path to revenue. The problem isn't your coding ability; it's your product focus.

User onboarding is the antidote to this malaise. By architecting a deliberate journey that guides users to their 'Aha!' moment within seconds, you stop building for yourself and start building for the market. This shift transforms your development process from a chore into a revenue-generating machine.

steps.title: Define the 'Aha!' Moment
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the single action that proves your product delivers value. This is the anchor for your entire onboarding flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a schema for a user onboarding flow that forces a user to complete one core action (e.g., generating a report) before accessing the dashboard.

steps.title: Implement Friction-Based Sign-up
steps.description: High-value users don't mind a 30-second survey. Ask two qualifying questions during sign-up to segment your audience and prioritize features.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a sign-up component that includes two custom fields for user intent and company size.

steps.title: Build the Interactive Walkthrough
steps.description: Use a guided tour to highlight the core value proposition. Keep it under three steps to avoid abandonment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a lightweight, non-intrusive modal walkthrough system for a React/Next.js application.

steps.title: Automate Success Triggers
steps.description: Use conditional logic to trigger emails or in-app nudges if the user stalls during the onboarding process.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a backend service that tracks onboarding completion and triggers a 'nudge' event if the user stops at step 2.

steps.title: Deploy and Measure Conversion
steps.description: Push your onboarding flow to production and use analytics to track how many users reach the final step.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Integrate a basic analytics hook into the onboarding completion function to log success rates.

pro_tips: Never ask for a credit card until the user has completed the onboarding 'Aha!' moment.
Use 'empty states' as a secondary onboarding tool—if there's no data, show a clear CTA to generate it.
Limit your MVP to one primary user journey; kill all secondary features until the first one converts.
Track 'Time-to-Value' (TTV) as your primary KPI; if it takes longer than 60 seconds, you are losing money.
Use micro-copy that speaks to the user's pain point, not the features of your software.
content.next_steps: Once your onboarding flow is live, spend 48 hours observing user behavior in your analytics dashboard. Identify where they drop off and iterate on the copy in that specific step.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in a vacuum and start shipping products that users actually want to pay for. Leverage the power of automated onboarding to validate your ideas in record time. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
